channel.title: Life-Cycles on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Life-Cycles on emsenn.net

item.title: Fungal Reproduction
item.description: Fungi reproduce through spores — small, durable cells released in enormous quantities and dispersed to colonize new substrates. A single fruiting body may release billions of spores over its lifespan. But fungal reproduction is more varied and more strange than this summary suggests. Fungi employ both sexual and asexual strategies, sometimes simultaneously. Their mating systems are among the most complex in biology. And the relationship between the hidden mycelium and the conspicuous fruiting body — the underground organism and its temporary reproductive structure — challenges intuitions about what an organism is and where reproduction begins.
